Warren Buffett Indicator at 205%, S&P 500 P/E ratio over 22, warning of stock market bubble by veteran investor Howard Marks amid high valuations.
news.summary: The stock market is showing signs of a possible bubble, with the Warren Buffett Indicator reaching a high of 205%, surpassing levels before the 2000 dot-com crash and 2007 financial crisis. The S&P 500's forward P/E ratio is over 22, indicating overvaluation. Howard Marks, the veteran investor who predicted the dot-com crash, is cautioning about high market valuations in a recent memo. Marks pointed out various warning signs, including market exuberance, reliance on a few key stocks, and a bias towards index investing. These indicators suggest a bubble may be forming in the market.

news.headline: Trump: No tariffs if manufacture or build product in the US.
Trump: EU lowered tariff to 2.5%. that's a big win.
Trump: We want to level playing field for all US companies.
Economists and betting markets predict mild January inflation: CPI to hold at 2.9%, with monthly increase of 0.3%.
news.summary: Economists and betting markets are currently predicting a mild January inflation report. The Consumer Price Index is expected to remain at 2.9% year-over-year, with a projected monthly increase of 0.3% (down from December's 0.4% rise). Core inflation, excluding food and energy prices, is anticipated to ease to 3.1% year-over-year, with a monthly increase of 0.3%. Markets are currently pricing in one rate cut by the end of the year, while there is a 50% probability of two cuts by December 2025. Betting markets similarly favor a 2.9% CPI reading, with odds of inflation exceeding 2.9% standing at 2-to-1. An unexpected inflation rate above 3.0% could yield a $53 return on a $10 wager.

news.headline: Fed Chair Powell says no hurry to cut interest rates, inflation somewhat elevated. Job gains average 189k/month, unemployment at 4%.
news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell recently addressed policymakers' stance on interest rates, emphasizing a cautious approach due to persistent inflation pressures. Despite making progress towards the Fed's 2% inflation target, Powell stated there is no rush to make adjustments. Speaking before the Senate Banking Committee, Powell acknowledged the strength of the economy, noting a monthly average of 189,000 payroll job gains over the last four months and an unchanged unemployment rate of 4% in January. Powell reassured that the labor market, although cooled, remains robust and not a significant source of inflationary pressures. He highlighted a decline in inflation over the past two years but noted that inflation levels are still somewhat elevated. The latest data revealed a 2.6% year-over-year increase in the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index as of December, with core PCE, excluding food, rising by 2.8%.

news.headline: US stocks dip as Powell remains cautious on rates. Intel climbs 7%, Tesla sinks to 11-week low. Markets await inflation data. Dow gains 0.1%.
news.summary: U.S. stocks slightly dropped as Federal Reserve Chair Powell expressed no rush to cut interest rates, affecting risk assets. Investors awaited the January Consumer Price Index report. S&P 500 fell by 0.1%, Nasdaq 100 by 0.2%, but Dow Jones gained 0.1%. Intel Corp. rose 7% post announcement on U.S. AI chips, while Tesla Inc. fell 4% due to weak Chinese sales. EU planned countermeasures against 25% U.S. tariffs, leading to higher long-dated Treasury yields at 4.54%. Dollar index fell by 0.2%. Full news on Benzinga.com.

Greenheart Gold expands Majorodam project in Suriname by 151.64 sq. km. Drilling begins at Heuvel target.
news.summary: In Longueuil, Quebec on February 13, 2025, Greenheart Gold Inc. (TSXV: GHRT) has expanded its exploration rights at the Majorodam project in Suriname. The original project covered 99.85 sq. km., but following agreements with two different title holders, it has now grown by 60.78 sq. km. to the north and 90.86 sq. km. to the south, totaling 251.5 sq. km. On February 12th, 2025, a reverse circulation drill rig arrived at the site, and drilling has begun at the Heuvel target.

AHF Poland marks International Condom Day to promote safe sex & raise awareness. More than 1 million STIs acquired daily globally.
news.summary: In Lublin, Poland, AIDS Healthcare Foundation (AHF) Poland is encouraging participation in International Condom Day (ICD), a festive occasion celebrating and promoting the use of condoms. These contraceptives are a straightforward and effective means of ensuring safety during sexual activities. Despite the alarming statistic of over 1 million sexually transmitted infections (STIs) acquired globally each day as reported by the World Health Organization, condom usage is decreasing in various regions. International Condom Day serves as an opportunity to raise awareness and explore innovative ways to encourage condom utilization.

news.headline: Survey of 90,000 reveals AI's impact on online romance, demand for authenticity. Largest human-only survey, reflects growing influence of AI on digital love.
news.summary: In San Francisco, a recent survey conducted by World unveiled insights on how AI is transforming modern romance, just in time for Valentine’s Day. With participation from over 90,000 individuals within the World Network, this massive human-only online survey stands as the largest of its kind. The results underscore the increasing impact of AI on digital romance, shedding light on a surging desire for genuine connections in virtual relationships. The survey delves into themes like engaging with chatbots and encountering fabricated matches, indicating a shift towards more authentic interactions in the realm of online dating.
